title: DataTable Rows.Add with PSObject-wrapped values causes InvalidCastException in typed columns
description: When adding rows to a System.Data.DataTable via .Rows.Add($val1, $val2, ...) OR by $row = $dt.NewRow(); $row[$colName] = $val, PowerShell wraps values in PSObject. If the DataTable column expects a specific .NET type (e.g. Boolean, Int32), the BooleanStorage.Set or Int32Storage.Set method receives PSObject instead of the raw type, causing 'System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object of type System.Management.Automation.PSObject to type System.IConvertible'. This occurs on untyped columns that infer types from first-row data, AND on correctly-typed columns when the value is not explicitly cast before assignment. The fix is to explicitly cast values: [bool]$val, [int]$val, [string]$val â€” or cast based on $dt.Columns[$colName].DataType in a loop. Two confirmed code vectors: (1) .Rows.Add() positional-arg shortcut; (2) $dt.NewRow() / $row[$col] = val without type cast.
detection_method: Search for .Rows.Add() calls where any argument comes from a PSCustomObject property (e.g. $item.enabled, $item.count) without explicit cast, AND the DataTable column was added without a type parameter.
known_instances: Show-CronAiAthonTool.ps1:715 â€” dt.Rows.Add($t.id, $t.name, $t.type, $t.enabled, ...) where $t.enabled is boolean from JSON but column is untyped
Main-GUI.ps1:391 â€” dgv.Rows.Add($false, $Key, ...) passes $false to DataGridViewCheckBoxColumn (less risky since $false is not from JSON but still PSObject-wrapped in some contexts)

Invoke-PSEnvironmentScanner.ps1:2148-2156 â€” Populate-GridByKey: columns typed correctly via TypeNameOfValue (e.g. [bool]) but $row[$p.Name] = $val assigns PSObject-wrapped value without explicit cast â€” BooleanStorage.Set / IntStorage.Set receive PSObject and throw InvalidCastException. SIN: SIN-20260412-SCANNER-POPULATEGRID-BOOLCAST
exception_message: System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object of type 'System.Management.Automation.PSObject' to type 'System.IConvertible' at System.Data.Common.BooleanStorage.Set(Int32 record, Object value)
remedy:
# Option 1: Add columns with explicit types
[void]$dt.Columns.Add('Enabled', [string])  # Store as string, not boolean

# Option 2: Cast values before adding rows
[void]$dt.Rows.Add([string]$t.id, [string]$t.name, [string]$t.type, [string]$t.enabled, ...)

# Option 3: Use .ToString() to unwrap PSObject
[void]$dt.Rows.Add($t.id.ToString(), $t.name.ToString(), ...)
preventionRule:
1. Always add DataTable columns with explicit type: $dt.Columns.Add('ColName', [string])
2. When populating rows from PSCustomObject properties, cast to [string] or the target .NET type.
3. Avoid passing raw boolean/numeric PSObject properties to untyped DataTable columns.
4. When using DataGridViewCheckBoxColumn, ensure the underlying DataTable column is typed [bool].
